semantic.role: Defines the formal schema for SpineRule documents within ArchSpine, enabling consistent validation and enforcement of architectural or behavioral policies.
responsibilities: Rule definition system (policy authoring and storage)
invariants: All SpineRule documents MUST include schemaVersion, ruleId, title, summary, appliesTo (array with at least one item), severity (one of advisory/warning/error), enforceable (boolean), and bodyMarkdown.
Additional properties beyond the defined ones are strictly prohibited (additionalProperties: false).
ruleId MUST conform to lowercase kebab-case pattern: ^[a-z0-9]+(?:-[a-z0-9]+)*$
schemaVersion MUST be a valid version string as defined in shared.schema.json
AppliesTo array must contain at least one non-empty string item.
Rationale is optional and can be null or a string.

English.role: This schema establishes the required structure and content validation rules for every SpineRule document used across ArchSpine. SpineRules are the backbone of policy-driven configuration – they define what patterns or behaviors are allowed, warned about, or rejected. The schema ensures that all rules are self-documenting (via bodyMarkdown), version-tracked (schemaVersion), and clearly scoped (appliesTo).

parameter_definitions.schemaVersion: Semantic version string from shared.schema. Controls which version of the rule format is in use; mismatches can break cross-system rule processing.
parameter_definitions.ruleId: Unique, human-readable identifier in kebab-case. Used for referencing and deduplication. Must be globally unique within the rule database.
parameter_definitions.title: Short, human-readable headline for the rule. Shown in dashboards and reports.
parameter_definitions.summary: Brief explanation of what the rule enforces or advises. Used in quick overviews and filtering.
parameter_definitions.appliesTo: Array of scoping tags (e.g., 'modulename', 'architecture-layer') that determine which artifacts this rule targets. At least one tag is required; empty lists are invalid.
parameter_definitions.severity: Enforcement level: advisory (informational), warning (non-blocking alert), error (blocking violation). Higher severity may halt builds or deployments.
parameter_definitions.enforceable: Boolean flag indicating whether automation should enforce this rule. False means the rule is for documentation or manual review only.
parameter_definitions.rationale: Optional reasoning behind the rule. Helps human readers understand intent; null allowed for concise rules.
parameter_definitions.bodyMarkdown: Markdown-formatted long description covering rule details, examples, exceptions, and remediation steps. Must be non-empty to ensure rules remain actionable.

English.stability_and_risks: The schema promotes strict consistency: all rules share an identical structure, reducing parsing errors. However, the invariants are rigid – if a rule document lacks any required field or adds an unsupported property, it will be rejected by validators. This could cause deployment failures if legacy rules are not updated. The severity enum prevents misuse of custom levels but limits flexibility. The pattern constraint on ruleId avoids spaces and special characters but rules authored with underscores or mixed case will fail validation. Overall, this schema improves system stability by enforcing a contract, but any change to it (e.g., adding new required fields) will break all existing rule documents until they are migrated.
